items.title: 'Not a proxy war': Sudan conflict 'would not go on like this' without international support
items.description: Pospisil says the siege has been ongoing for almost a year, with the RSF appearing determined to seize el-Obeid. While he argues that Sudan's civil war is not a proxy war in the strict sense, he also makes clear that "without international support, the war would not go on like this." Yet none of the external actors involved, whether the UAE, which supplies arms to the RSF, or the US and Saudi Arabia, has the leverage to bring the warring parties to a ceasefire, let alone a negotiated settlement.

While the United States marks the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with rallies, parties and fireworks, history’s first US-born pope traveled to the Sicilian island of Lampedusa to pray at a migrant cemetery and celebrate a solemn Mass for the island's newest arrivals. items.description: A man believed to be in his 60s died after being pulled from a room engulfed in flames in a Springvale South home overnight.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Putin & The ‘Drowning Man Syndrome’ Sudan
items.description: From intensifying drone warfare between Ukraine and Russia to mounting fears of a humanitarian catastrophe in Sudan, anti-immigration protests in South Africa and a landmark ruling by the US Supreme Court, this week has been shaped by conflicts over power, identity and international law.

items.title: Banned by Beijing, this comedian is taking his act to Chinese speakers abroad
items.description: Chizi, one of China’s biggest stand-up comedians, has found a new stage after falling afoul of China's censors.

items.title: Ex-HK bookseller Lam Wing-kee, detained by China in 2015, dies in Taiwan at 70
items.description: Lam was one of five people from Causeway Bay Books who disappeared in 2015. He later detailed his detention by Chinese authorities.

items.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
items.description: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.

items.title: Interpol names suspect in Monaco bombing that reportedly targeted Russia-linked Ukrainian tycoon
items.description: Suspect of the June 29 blasts in Monaco subject to an arrest warrant.

items.title: Iran gets ready for Khamenei's funeral
items.description: He was assassinated in a #US-Israeli strike in February 2026. #Iran's Supreme leader Ali #Khamenei's #funerals are set to be held on July 4, with authorities expecting between 15 and 20 million people to attend.

items.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
items.description: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

items.title: Experts: Hantavirus detected in Tierra del Fuego, but no link to cruise outbreak
items.description: Experts detect hantavirus in rodents captured in Tierra del Fuego Province, although the strain differs from the one responsible for the outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship. Leer más

items.title: What will guests wear to Taylor Swift's  wedding?
items.description: Romantic, fairytale vibes could dominate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's reported wedding event in New York City on Friday.

items.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
items.description: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

items.title: Egypt makes history, heartbreak for Cape Verde at World Cup
items.description: Heartbreak for Cape Verde as the World Cup debutants bowed out after a thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina. The Blue Sharks twice battled back to level the match, pushing Lionel Messi's side to the limit in one of the tournament's biggest surprise performances.

items.title: Trump extols America, rails at communism as U.S. turns 250
items.description: “A generation after we fought and won the Cold War against the menace of communism, there is now a resurgence of the communist menace in our land,” Trump said

items.title: More than 400 dead in DR Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.description: In tonight's edition, Ebola is spreading beyond its original hotspot in the DRC as a new case has been detected nearly 600 kilometers away. And another human rights catastrophe is unfolding in Sudan around the besieged city of al-Obeid, the UN says.

items.title: No need for India to take a leaf out of Pakistan’s Iran mediation book, says envoy
items.description: Mediating in an “already crowded” field on the Iran crisis would not have benefited India, said Ambassador to China Vikram Doraiswami

items.title: Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. And, Vatican excommunicates rebel group
items.description: Trump and his family earned over $1 billion last year through cryptocurrency ventures and other businesses. And, the Vatican declared that the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X has entered schism.

items.title: U.S. withdrew forces from Nigeria after operation against ISIS, AFRICOM chief says
items.description: AFRICOM Commander General Dagvin Anderson described May’s joint U.S.-Nigerian as a ​model for future security cooperation ‌in Africa

items.title: Nurse says psychiatrist downplayed Maradona's crisis during home hospitalisation
items.description: Diego Maradona suffered a sudden change in mood that was downplayed by his psychiatrist during the period of home hospitalisation in which he died, claims nurse in testimony. Leer más

items.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce are married in elaborate Madison Square Garden ceremony
items.description: Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce officially tied the knot on the night of July 3, 2026, at Madison Square Garden. With actor Adam Sandler stepping in as the surprising officiant

items.title: U.S. Fourth of July celebrations: Dangerous heat wave forces cancellation of Independence Day parade in Washington
items.description: Around 160 million Americans were under either major or extreme heat warnings as the country prepared to mark the 250th anniversary of its independence, according to the National Weather Service

items.title: DR Congo opens Ebola nurseries to protect children separated from infected parents
items.description: As the Democratic Republic of the Congo battles a worsening Ebola outbreak, authorities have established special nurseries to care for children whose parents are receiving treatment. The centres provide shelter, daily care and psychological support while helping reduce the risk of infection.

items.title: China accuses critics of ethnic unity law of 'spreading falsehoods'
items.description: Mr. Guo's comments came after nine United States lawmakers voiced stern opposition to the law, pledging in a statement to keep speaking out against Beijing's bid to "legitimise its transnational repression"

items.title: Venezuela quake survivor pulled out alive after eight days
items.description: Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park.

items.title: Argentina's economy contracted in April as uneven growth persists
items.description: Economic activity fell 1.5% from March; From a year ago, gross domestic product proxy grew 1.6%. Leer más

items.title: Watch: INDIA bloc writes to CJI over SIR, EC misconduct | Above the Fold | 03.07.2026
items.description: The episode looks at the INDIA bloc’s joint memorandum to the Chief Justice of India; the Allahabad High Court’s stance on the Places of Worship Act; Russia-Ukraine war; former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei state funeral, Uddhav Thackeray’s “Ram Raksha” protest, latest FIFA updates, and more.

items.title: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz remains a powerful bargaining chip
items.description: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: Brazil's Lula blasts Flavio Bolsonaro for requesting U.S. tariff delay
items.description: The delay would prevent the rate hike from being interpreted "as an attempt to influence the outcome" of the elections, Lula’s right-wing rival said.

items.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, what is 'deadly' and what does it mean to you?
items.description: As official NAIDOC celebrations get underway across the country, the ABC's Matt Francis asks what is deadly and what does it mean to the Indigenous community across the country.

items.title: Asian markets rise as beaten tech stocks enjoy respite from selling
items.description: Oil prices edged up but held recent losses fuelled by increased traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and on hopes for progress in US-Iran talks.

items.title: Milei-linked Fundación Faro told to name donors behind US$4.8mn windfall
items.description: Fundación Faro presented itself as a platform of ideas. But the libertarian think tank’s opaque finances, links to key Milei insiders and 400-fold surge in donations have left the organisation facing uncomfortable questions from regulators and judges. Leer más

items.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
items.description: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.

items.title: German row over plan for workers to need sick note on first day of illness
items.description: A doctors' group says it "borders on madness" that patients will have to obtain the note in person.

items.title: Lufthansa expects challenging second half of 2026 but sees growth in Asia Pacific
items.description: The German airline group tells CNA’s Yasmin Jonkers that geopolitical tensions, higher costs and sustainability pressures will continue, even as demand in the Asia-Pacific region remains strong.

items.title: Arias sends Colombia into World Cup last-16 with 1-0 win over Ghana
items.description: The World Cup round of 32 ends with Colombia beating Ghana 1-0, as Jhon Arias sends the South Americans through.

items.title: Accra and Pretoria at loggerheads over death of Ghanaian national
items.description: A diplomatic row is brewing between Ghana and South Africa over the death of a Ghanaian national in Cape Town.

items.title: Ahead of Morocco vs Canada at the World Cup, comparisons arise with the 2022 team
items.description: Despite the ups and downs between the two World Cups, Morocco have once again risen as the top Arab and African team.

items.title: Iran: Khamenei's funeral ceremonies officially begin
items.description: Huge crowds have gathered in Tehran to pay their final respects to Iran's supreme leader, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran.

items.title: WHO says 120 dead in Sudan's latest cholera outbreak
items.description: The UN health body says another 1,102 suspected cases have been reported since May in isolated war zones across the country.

items.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei funeral: ‘Revenge, revenge’, Iran mourners chant as they gather
items.description: Authorities unveiled the casket containing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's body in a glass case before the weeping Mourners

items.title: Javier Milei taps lifetime politician Diego Santilli to lead his Cabinet after scandal
items.description: Javier Milei once vowed to eradicate entrenched politicians like Diego Santilli, yet he turned to him as a candidate and later named him interior minister. Now, the former PRO politician is Argentina's new Cabinet chief. Leer más
